page: Microsoft Azure HB176-144rs_v4 is a high-performance computing server instance belonging to the HBv4 server family. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel processor. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 5.33 GB of RAM per core. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Additionally, the instance is RDMA capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput networking interfaces suitable for clustered environments. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Given its high core count, substantial memory capacity, and integrated local storage, this instance offers high resource density for demanding computational tasks. It is designed for workloads requiring significant parallel processing capabilities and fast local storage access. Appropriate workloads include high-performance computing simulations, large-scale database deployments, and intensive data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure HB176-144rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server within the HBv4 family, designed for high-performance computing workloads. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding 5.33 GB of RAM per core, alongside 4332 GB of local storage. It supports Premium Storage and is RDMA capable, facilitating high-speed, low-latency networking for clustered configurations. With no integrated GPU, this server is optimized for CPU-intensive parallel processing. The combination of high core density, substantial memory, and fast local storage makes this instance highly efficient for demanding computational tasks. It is well-suited for workloads such as large-scale scientific simulations, database management, and complex data processing pipelines.
